acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplRig no longer relies on check-then-bind port selection for daemon or proxy listeners
- [ ] Tailnet and repl integration tests stay isolated under parallel nextest runs
- [ ] Canonical fast and slow tiers stay green after the port-allocation change
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs` allocates daemon and proxy listen ports with `pick_port()`: bind `127.0.0.1:0`, read the port, drop the listener, then bind that address for real later. Under parallel nextest runs another test can claim the same port in the gap, turning replication and proxy setup into a race.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/bin/tailnet_proxy.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/repl/server.rs`
design: Stop probing free ports up front. Teach the harness to use actual bound sockets or `127.0.0.1:0` end-to-end, then publish the real listen address back into fixture metadata/config before peers reload replication. The fix should remove the check-then-bind gap for both daemon listeners and tailnet proxies.
title: Eliminate ReplRig check-then-bind port races in replication harness
